criteria.description: --json flag outputs each matching entry as a JSON object, one per line
JSON output is valid — re-parseable by a JSON parser
JSON output works with both search and summary subcommands

evidence.AC_1: search --json iterates entries calling FormatEntryJSON+Println (one JSON object per line). TestOU2_SearchJSONOutput confirms 5 lines for 5 entries. TestOU2_SearchJSONWithFilter confirms 1 line for filtered result.
evidence.AC_2: FormatEntryJSON uses encoding/json.Marshal (stdlib). TestOU2_SearchJSONValid re-parses every output line with json.Unmarshal, fails on invalid JSON. TestOU2_SummaryJSONOutput also re-parses summary JSON.
evidence.AC_3: search subcommand: --json flag at search.go:29, used at search.go:133. summary subcommand: --json flag at main.go:54, calls FormatJSON() at main.go:87. Both tested: TestOU2_SearchJSONOutput and TestOU2_SummaryJSONOutput.
